speaker: CAESAR
text: Go to him, Dolabella, bid him yield.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Being so frustrate, tell him, he mocks

speaker: CAESAR
text: The pauses that he makes.

speaker: DOLABELLA
text: Caesar, I shall.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Wherefore is that? And what art thou that dar’st

speaker: CAESAR
text: Appear thus to us?

speaker: DERCETUS
text: I am called Dercetus.

speaker: DERCETUS
text: Mark Antony I served, who best was worthy

speaker: DERCETUS
text: Best to be served. Whilst he stood up and spoke,

speaker: DERCETUS
text: He was my master, and I wore my life

speaker: DERCETUS
text: To spend upon his haters. If thou please

speaker: DERCETUS
text: To take me to thee, as I was to him

speaker: DERCETUS
text: I’ll be to Caesar; if thou pleasest not,

speaker: DERCETUS
text: I yield thee up my life.

speaker: CAESAR
text: What is ’t thou say’st?

speaker: DERCETUS
text: I say, O Caesar, Antony is dead.

speaker: CAESAR
text: The breaking of so great a thing should make

speaker: CAESAR
text: A greater crack. The round world

speaker: CAESAR
text: Should have shook lions into civil streets

speaker: CAESAR
text: And citizens to their dens. The death of Antony

speaker: CAESAR
text: Is not a single doom; in the name lay

speaker: CAESAR
text: A moiety of the world.

speaker: DERCETUS
text: He is dead, Caesar,

speaker: DERCETUS
text: Not by a public minister of justice,

speaker: DERCETUS
text: Nor by a hirèd knife, but that self hand

speaker: DERCETUS
text: Which writ his honor in the acts it did

speaker: DERCETUS
text: Hath, with the courage which the heart did lend it,

speaker: DERCETUS
text: Splitted the heart. This is his sword.

speaker: DERCETUS
text: I robbed his wound of it. Behold it stained

speaker: DERCETUS
text: With his most noble blood.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Look you sad, friends?

speaker: CAESAR
text: The gods rebuke me, but it is tidings

speaker: CAESAR
text: To wash the eyes of kings.

speaker: AGRIPPA
text: And strange it is

speaker: AGRIPPA
text: That nature must compel us to lament

speaker: AGRIPPA
text: Our most persisted deeds.

speaker: MAECENAS
text: His taints and honors

speaker: MAECENAS
text: Waged equal with him.

speaker: AGRIPPA
text: A rarer spirit never

speaker: AGRIPPA
text: Did steer humanity, but you gods will give us

speaker: AGRIPPA
text: Some faults to make us men. Caesar is touched.

speaker: MAECENAS
text: When such a spacious mirror’s set before him,

speaker: MAECENAS
text: He needs must see himself.

speaker: CAESAR
text: O Antony,

speaker: CAESAR
text: I have followed thee to this, but we do lance

speaker: CAESAR
text: Diseases in our bodies. I must perforce

speaker: CAESAR
text: Have shown to thee such a declining day

speaker: CAESAR
text: Or look on thine. We could not stall together

speaker: CAESAR
text: In the whole world. But yet let me lament

speaker: CAESAR
text: With tears as sovereign as the blood of hearts

speaker: CAESAR
text: That thou my brother, my competitor

speaker: CAESAR
text: In top of all design, my mate in empire,

speaker: CAESAR
text: Friend and companion in the front of war,

speaker: CAESAR
text: The arm of mine own body, and the heart

speaker: CAESAR
text: Where mine his thoughts did kindle—that our stars

speaker: CAESAR
text: Unreconciliable should divide

speaker: CAESAR
text: Our equalness to this. Hear me, good friends—

speaker: CAESAR
text: But I will tell you at some meeter season.

speaker: CAESAR
text: The business of this man looks out of him.

speaker: CAESAR
text: We’ll hear him what he says.—Whence are you?

speaker: EGYPTIAN
text: A poor Egyptian yet, the Queen my mistress,

speaker: EGYPTIAN
text: Confined in all she has, her monument,

speaker: EGYPTIAN
text: Of thy intents desires instruction,

speaker: EGYPTIAN
text: That she preparedly may frame herself

speaker: EGYPTIAN
text: To th’ way she’s forced to.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Bid her have good heart.

speaker: CAESAR
text: She soon shall know of us, by some of ours,

speaker: CAESAR
text: How honorable and how kindly we

speaker: CAESAR
text: Determine for her. For Caesar cannot live

speaker: CAESAR
text: To be ungentle.

speaker: EGYPTIAN
text: So the gods preserve thee.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Come hither, Proculeius. Go and say

speaker: CAESAR
text: We purpose her no shame. Give her what comforts

speaker: CAESAR
text: The quality of her passion shall require,

speaker: CAESAR
text: Lest, in her greatness, by some mortal stroke

speaker: CAESAR
text: She do defeat us, for her life in Rome

speaker: CAESAR
text: Would be eternal in our triumph. Go,

speaker: CAESAR
text: And with your speediest bring us what she says

speaker: CAESAR
text: And how you find of her.

speaker: PROCULEIUS
text: Caesar, I shall.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Gallus, go you along.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Where’s Dolabella,

speaker: CAESAR
text: To second Proculeius?

speaker: ALL
text: Dolabella!

speaker: CAESAR
text: Let him alone, for I remember now

speaker: CAESAR
text: How he’s employed. He shall in time be ready.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Go with me to my tent, where you shall see

speaker: CAESAR
text: How hardly I was drawn into this war,

speaker: CAESAR
text: How calm and gentle I proceeded still

speaker: CAESAR
text: In all my writings. Go with me and see

speaker: CAESAR
text: What I can show in this.
